topic: Someone finds their college application essay published as an example
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the screen, his breath catching in his throat. The document, his meticulously crafted narrative about overcoming a childhood fear through competitive chess, was sitting there, perfectly formatted, with a caption beneath it: "Example Essay: Resilience in Action." His hands began to tremble, the edges of the paper blurring slightly under his frantic grip. He reread the words, the earnest passion he had poured into them now feeling exposed, laid bare for everyone to scrutinize. A slow, hot flush crept up his neck, staining his cheeks crimson. He quickly minimized the window, his gaze darting around his dorm room as if expecting someone to materialize and witness his utter undoing. The silence of the room amplified the frantic thumping in his chest.

[story 2]

"No. No, this can't be happening." Sarah whispered, her voice tight with disbelief as she traced the words on the screen. The essay, her deeply personal reflection on finding solace in urban gardening, was displayed prominently, labeled as a model submission. Her jaw tightened, and she instinctively pressed her palms against her eyes, as if the sight itself were too much to bear. A wave of intense heat washed over her face, making her skin prickle uncomfortably. She snatched the laptop shut with a sharp, jerky motion, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet library. Every muscle in her body seemed to lock up, leaving her feeling suddenly clumsy and intensely aware of every movement she made.

[story 3]

Mark leaned back in his chair, his eyes wide and unblinking as he absorbed the screen. His carefully constructed story about his journey through volunteer work, the quiet dedication he showed to an animal shelter, was displayed as a benchmark piece. A strange, hollow sensation settled in his stomach, a heavy, sickening drop. He slowly lowered his gaze from the text, his shoulders slumping forward. He cleared his throat, a dry, ragged sound, and began to nervously adjust his glasses, avoiding any direct eye contact with the glowing rectangle. He fiddled incessantly with the edge of his notebook, his fingers tracing meaningless patterns on the cover, wishing desperately that the moment could simply rewind itself.
